title: atriplex canescens subsp. canescens
intro: Atriplex canescens subsp. canescens, also known as four-winged saltbush, is a shrub species in the family Amaranthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Atriplex canescens subsp. canescens can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
